claims.statement: The four-dimensional context exhibits replicated representational geometry and held-out-source factor information.
Prototype-energy routing improves nominal route order over the MLP on trained and held-out factor grids.
Prototype-energy routing improves functional route order on trained factors, but not on held-out factors.
A low-capacity linear router is at least as competitive as prototype-energy routing and shows the strongest causal and ablation signals.
Functional causal specificity is not replicated for prototype-energy routing.
Routing territories do not yet establish stable functional host specialization.
Routing changes remain within the preregistered operational non-degradation margin.
No operational superiority claim is supported.
Continual route-function memory transport is not implemented.

reviewer_flags: Functional route mediation generalizes nominally but not under the method-specific functional metric on held-out factors.
Method-specific host cost matrices confound router movement with host-function changes.
Prototype territories are sharper but do not pass causal specialization or prediction-identity gates.
Linear routing deserves equal status in v1.1.1.
next_required: Freeze a common host bank and common C* cost matrix.
Compare MLP, linear, prototype, and hybrid directional-prototype routers.
Promote held-out functional mediation to the primary gate.
Add local continuity distributions, perturbation maps, route swaps, and candidate-permutation tests.
